title: COSO Principle 11 — Technology general controls: IT change-management and configuration-drift monitoring
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=COSO, clause=Principle11, available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Phase-C authored UC targeting COSO Principle 11 (Selects and develops general controls over technology). Monitors IT change management and configuration drift; assurance level set to 'contributing' pending SME review per Phase E of the regulation-coverage plan.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that COSO Principle 11 (Selects and develops general controls over technology) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.50.33: COSO Principle 11 — Technology general controls: IT change-management and configuration-drift monitoring.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.33' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: Change-management ticket systems (ServiceNow/Jira), configuration-management database (CMDB), configuration-compliance scans.
spl:
index=change_mgmt sourcetype IN (change_tickets, config_drift) earliest=-30d
| eval unauthorised=if(sourcetype="config_drift" AND isnotnull(drift_detected) AND isnull(change_ticket_ref), 1, 0)
| eval approved=if(sourcetype="change_tickets" AND approval_status="approved", 1, 0)
| eval emergency=if(change_type="emergency", 1, 0)
| stats count AS total_changes sum(unauthorised) AS unauthorised_changes sum(approved) AS approved_changes sum(emergency) AS emergency_changes dc(affected_ci) AS affected_assets BY environment, change_category
| eval approval_pct=round(100*approved_changes/total_changes, 1)
| where unauthorised_changes>0 OR approval_pct<100
| table environment, change_category, total_changes, approved_changes, unauthorised_changes, emergency_changes, approval_pct, affected_assets
description: Monitors IT change-management approval rates and detects configuration drift without corresponding change tickets, providing evidence of COSO Principle 11 technology general controls.
value: COSO Principle 11 requires selection and development of general controls over technology. Continuous change-management and configuration-drift monitoring provides auditable evidence that IT general controls are effective.
implementation: (1) Ingest change-ticket and config-drift events with change_ticket_ref, approval_status, change_type; (2) correlate drift with change tickets; (3) schedule daily; (4) escalate unauthorised changes. Indexes required: `index=change_mgmt`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=change_tickets`, `sourcetype=config_drift`.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `affected_assets`, `approval_pct`, `approved`, `approved_changes`, `change_category`, `emergency`.
knownFalsePositives: Tuning prerequisites: source field mappings must be in place before the UC will produce reliable metrics; early runs will commonly flag unmapped systems until onboarding is completed.
grandmaExplanation: We keep track of monitors it change-management approval rates and detects configuration drift without corresponding change tickets, providing evidence of coso principle 11 technology general controls. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Change-management ticket systems (ServiceNow/Jira), configuration-management database (CMDB), configuration-compliance scans.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest change-ticket and config-drift events with change_ticket_ref, approval_status, change_type; (2) correlate drift with change tickets; (3) schedule daily; (4) escalate unauthorised changes.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=change_mgmt sourcetype IN (change_tickets, config_drift) earliest=-30d
| eval unauthorised=if(sourcetype="config_drift" AND isnotnull(drift_detected) AND isnull(change_ticket_ref), 1, 0)
| eval approved=if(sourcetype="change_tickets" AND approval_status="approved", 1, 0)
| eval emergency=if(change_type="emergency", 1, 0)
| stats count AS total_changes sum(unauthorised) AS unauthorised_changes sum(approved) AS approved_changes sum(emergency) AS emergency_changes dc(affected_ci) AS affected_assets BY environment, change_category
| eval approval_pct=round(100*approved_changes/total_changes, 1)
| where unauthorised_changes>0 OR approval_pct<100
| table environment, change_category, total_changes, approved_changes, unauthorised_changes, emergency_changes, approval_pct, affected_assets
```

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
